name: quick-backup-restore
description: Restic-powered encrypted time machine for OpenClaw: hourly incremental snapshots, fast restore by time/snapshot/file, local-only privacy defaults, integrity checks, retention, and optional alerts. Setup can run with sudo, install dependencies, create a systemd timer or cron job, restore files that may overwrite current state, and prune or purge old recovery data.

filesystem: restore snapshots into a chosen target, including / when explicitly confirmed
forget and prune old snapshots according to retention settings
optionally purge repository, password file, and logs during uninstall
system: optionally installs apt packages and yq
optionally creates systemd timer/service or cron job
optionally writes logrotate configuration and lock/marker files
network: Telegram failure notifications are optional and disabled by default
healthcheck pings are optional and disabled by default
ClawHub update checks are optional and disabled by default
privacy.local_only blocks all external integrations by default
sensitive_data: OpenClaw memory, sessions, configs, and user-added paths may contain secrets
credential stores such as ~/.ssh and ~/.gnupg are not auto-suggested and require manual opt-in
